key_quotes: You're required to for the first two years. But you can for all four. A lot of ppl move off campus after the first two into apts nearby and walk to campus.
Shaw is the dorm that's closest to studio...heated sidewalks for the walk to campus on a snowy day
Don't do Shaw its considered the worst dorm socially unless ur vibe is more of the less social ppl
key_points: Two-year dorm requirement; most students move off-campus after.
North and south campus offer distinct living experiences.
Shaw Hall has pod bathrooms and in-building dining but feels isolating.

key_quotes: If you live on South or join Greek life, some sororities have their own chef

incredible school spirit, great academics
key_points: Greek life dominates the social scene; not ideal for everyone.
Party culture is strong, with some sororities having private chefs.
Roommate boundary violations, like uninvited guests staying months, are reported.

key_quotes: It's gonna be cafeteria food. Basically what you've been eating the past 14 years of your life in school. Just with more choices.
If you aren't super busy then BBB and Graham are worth the walk.
Doordash gets expensive, dont rely on that.
key_points: BBB and Graham are the best dining halls on campus.
Standard cafeteria quality overall; location determines convenience.
DoorDash is popular but becomes a costly habit quickly.

General stress and anxiety themes appear but lack SU context.
Mental health support infrastructure remains unclear from student feedback.

key_points: Aid is negotiable; meeting reps or appealing can yield more.
Proactive outreach to regional representatives produces the best results.
Aid has limits; persistence matters but outcomes aren't guaranteed.

key_quotes: Getting an internship in CE/CS as a freshman is extremely uncommon especially at a big company.

Joining an LLC lets you immediately connect with other people in your major and a faculty member which may be helpful later on down the road when looking for internships and letters of recommendation.
Syracuse University (Direct admit to Journalism)
key_points: Journalism, architecture, Maxwell, and communications are standout programs.
Architecture is intense; students routinely sleep in studios.
Meaningful tech internships typically emerge junior year, not freshman year.

financial aid is not infinite
key_points: Financial aid office is responsive to students who reach out proactively.
RA oversight is inconsistent; one RA was absent most weekends.
Limited broader administrative feedback exists in student reviews.

heated sidewalks for the walk to campus on a snowy day

key_points: Campus facilities are among the best at this tier of school.
Heated sidewalks make harsh Syracuse winters more manageable.
Surrounding city is considered underwhelming but offers basic amenities.

key_quotes: Most students land internships in the summer between sophomore/junior year and the summer between junior/senior year.
There are many, many opportunities for computer engineering majors in many industries

CS and engineering students face a highly competitive job market.
Most students secure meaningful internships between sophomore and junior years.

Less suited for students seeking quiet or a true urban environment.
